items.q: What is the rough formula for maximum heart rate?
items.why: Students misremember the formula. 220 - age is the classic estimate (rough; varies individually). A 20-year-old's estimated max HR is around 200 bpm; an 80-year-old's is around 140.

items.q: What does the 'M' in SMART goals stand for?
items.why: Students guess from common psychology terms. SMART = Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic (or Relevant), Time-bound. Measurable means progress can be tracked (e.g., '5 km in 25 minutes' not 'get faster').

items.correct: Information the performer senses from their own body during/after movement.
items.why: Students reverse the meaning. Intrinsic = from WITHIN (kinesthetic awareness, feel); extrinsic = from OUTSIDE (coach feedback, video, statistics). Experienced performers rely more on intrinsic feedback.
The answer examiners want is: Cardio endurance. “Strength only” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Multiple stations. “One exercise” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many training methods were named (continuous, interval, circuit, weight)?
items.why: Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
